prompt: The method should check if array is sorted ascending but returns true for {3,1,2}. What is the bug?
choices: The comparison arr[i] > arr[i+1] should be arr[i] >= arr[i+1] for strict sorting
i < arr.length - 2 stops one pair short; the last two elements are never compared, so {1,2,3,1} would return true
The loop should iterate backwards from arr.length - 1
isSorted should use Arrays.sort() and compare the result
explanation: The loop bound arr.length - 2 means the last index checked is arr.length - 3, comparing arr[length-3] with arr[length-2]. The pair (arr[length-2], arr[length-1]) is never examined. An array like {1,2,0} would incorrectly return true. Fix: i < arr.length - 1.
